labels.label: reference
labels.description: Content organized to provide lookup information, definitions, facts, entries, glossaries, encyclopedic summaries, manuals, or structured explanatory reference material.
positive_examples: An encyclopedia-style entry about a plant species.
A glossary definition with concise factual explanation.
A knowledge-base page summarizing a concept or entity.
negative_notes: API-specific reference should usually be documentation_api.
A tutorial with step-by-step instruction should be education_tutorial.
Reference is a function, not a semantic topic; assign semantic_topic_domain separately.

labels.label: education_tutorial
labels.description: Instructional content intended to teach, explain, train, or guide a learner through concepts, exercises, lessons, examples, worksheets, courses, or how-to steps.
positive_examples: A lesson explaining photosynthesis to students.
A step-by-step programming tutorial.
A worksheet or study guide for algebra.
negative_notes: Do not use this label for every clear explanation; require pedagogical framing or instructional structure.
Official API docs may be documentation_api even if educational.
Education is not a semantic topic; assign the subject separately.

labels.label: news_media
labels.description: Journalistic, broadcast, magazine, interview, report, press, or media-style content focused on events, current affairs, public communication, reviews, or reported narratives.
positive_examples: A news article about an election, product launch, or scientific discovery.
An interview transcript published by a media outlet.
A magazine-style report about climate, culture, or business.
negative_notes: Blog opinion pieces without journalistic reporting may be blog_opinion.
Press releases selling a product may be product_commercial.
Media is a genre/function label, not a subject domain.

labels.label: forum_qa
labels.description: Question-and-answer, forum, discussion thread, support exchange, comments, troubleshooting conversation, or community response format.
positive_examples: A Stack Overflow-style question with answers.
A forum thread troubleshooting an error.
A community Q&A about health, travel, or consumer products.
negative_notes: A polished FAQ page from a company may be reference or product_commercial depending on intent.
Do not infer low quality from forum format alone.
Semantic topic depends on the content of the question and answers.

labels.label: blog_opinion
labels.description: Personal, editorial, review, commentary, essay, reflective, or opinionated web content where the author's perspective or narrative stance is prominent.
positive_examples: A personal blog post reviewing a book or gadget.
An opinion essay about public policy.
A reflective post about productivity, travel, or culture.
negative_notes: Reported news should usually be news_media.
Step-by-step teaching posts may be education_tutorial.
Opinionated tone does not determine semantic topic.

labels.label: product_commercial
labels.description: Content whose primary function is selling, promoting, listing, pricing, advertising, lead generation, affiliate conversion, service marketing, or product/service comparison for commercial action.
positive_examples: A product page for an industrial sensor with price and call-to-action.
A SaaS pricing or feature marketing page.
An e-commerce listing, affiliate review, or service landing page.
negative_notes: Commercial intent is not a semantic topic; assign the product's subject separately.
A neutral technical specification may be reference if not primarily promotional.
Spammy commercial pages should also be captured by quality_noise where appropriate.

labels.label: documentation_api
labels.description: Developer or technical documentation for APIs, commands, configuration, libraries, SDKs, protocols, data formats, software options, or system behavior.
positive_examples: A function reference for a Python method.
CLI command documentation with flags and examples.
A configuration guide for a database, web server, or cloud SDK.
negative_notes: General programming tutorials may be education_tutorial.
Non-software product manuals may be reference or engineering documentation, depending on future schema needs.
Semantic topic is usually computer_science_software, but still record it separately.

labels.label: academic_paper_like
labels.description: Research-paper-like content with abstract, methodology, formal results, citations, experiments, theorem/proof structure, or scholarly exposition.
positive_examples: An abstract describing experimental methods and findings.
A paper excerpt with citations, sections, and results.
A theorem/proof style mathematical manuscript.
negative_notes: Educational summaries of research may be education_tutorial or reference.
News reports about papers should be news_media.
Academic style can appear in any semantic topic.

labels.label: list_table_directory
labels.description: Content primarily structured as a list, table, catalog, index, directory, registry, menu-like collection, ranking, or repeated record set.
positive_examples: A directory of offices, products, terms, or organizations.
A table of measurements, model names, prices, or locations.
A ranked list or catalog with repeated entries.
negative_notes: A table embedded in an otherwise explanatory article does not necessarily define the whole genre.
Commercial catalogs may also be product_commercial; choose the dominant function for v1.
The listed entities determine semantic topic when recoverable.

labels.label: boilerplate_navigation
labels.description: Navigation, menus, cookie text, footer material, login prompts, legal fragments, repeated site chrome, extraction residue, or other page scaffolding rather than main content.
positive_examples: A chunk dominated by header links, footer links, and cookie prompts.
Repeated subscription, sign-in, or share-widget text.
Terms/navigation residue with little main article content.
negative_notes: Legal or policy content with substantive rules may be reference or news_media depending on function.
Use quality_noise fields to capture extraction noise severity.
Do not use this label for a clean table of contents that is part of a useful document unless it dominates the chunk.

labels.label: other_function
labels.description: Use when the document function is unclear, mixed, outside the current genre/function taxonomy, or not recoverable from the chunk.
positive_examples: A short fragment with no clear communicative format.
A mixed chunk combining unrelated snippets without a dominant function.
A valid main-content passage whose genre is not covered by the current labels.
negative_notes: Prefer a specific function when one is clearly dominant.
Do not use other_function to represent semantic uncertainty; that belongs to semantic_topic_domain or abstention.
Do not use other_function as a quality label.
